title: Pink Pony Club
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I know you wanted me to stay
  > [#1, 19 votes] In the opening lyric of “Pink Pony Club,” Chappell Roan addresses her mother’s expectations for her. She struggles with her family’s desires to stay in her Midwest hometown — a concept that is...
But I can't ignore the crazy visions of me in L.A.
  > [#2, 36 votes] Chappell had gone to LA for a little while back before she had blown up as an artist. She said that the space felt so comforting and welcoming, particularly to the queer community. In her words, she...
And I heard that there's a special place
  > [#3, 74 votes] According to Roan, this special place is West Hollywood’s iconic gay bar The Abbey, which features male and female go-go dancers and regular drag shows. In 2023, she talked with Teen Vogue about her...
Where boys and girls can all be queens every single day

[Refrain]
I'm having wicked dreams of leaving Tennessee
  > [#4, 73 votes] Despite naming Tennessee in the refrain, Roan actually grew up in Willard, Missouri. She talked a bit about her experience growing up there in a 2022 profile with Rolling Stone: I felt so out of...
Hear Santa Monica, I swear it's calling me
Won't make my mama proud, it's gonna cause a scene
  > [#5, 36 votes] Here Roan touches on a painful experience familiar to many LGBTQ+ people raised in conservative families: To be true to herself as a queer person, she risks disappointment and rejection from her...
She sees her baby girl, I know she's gonna scream
  > [#6, 25 votes] “Baby girl” shows her mother still viewing her as a baby and not the women she grew to be. The first line is basically saying when her southern conservative mother sees her as this person she’s never...

[Pre-Chorus]
"God, what have you done?
  > [#7, 73 votes] While being a “pink pony girl” here means that she dances at the Pink Pony Club, a pink pony is a stereotypical interest for a little girl in a place like Tennessee with strong horse riding and...
You're a pink pony girl, and you dance at the club," oh mama
  > [#8, 35 votes] She is trying to explain to her mother who hates her lifestyle that she’s living this way because she enjoys it and is having fun by living this way. Her mother would view her lifestyle as a dancer...
I'm just having fun
On the stage in my heels, it's where I belong, down at the
  > [#9, 63 votes] Moving to Los Angeles in 2018 allowed Roan to live freely as a queer woman for the first time in her life. Roan found a sense of belonging outside her biological family within queer spaces, a common...

[Chorus]
Pink Pony Club, I'm gonna keep on dancing at the
West Hollywood, I'm gonna keep on dancing at the
  > [#10, 74 votes] In a 2023 interview with Teen Vogue, Roan stated that the eponymous Pink Pony Club has become a real space through the crowd of her live shows, where drag queens are the openers and which are...
Pink Pony Club, I'm gonna keep on dancing down in
  > [#11, 43 votes] West Hollywood is a city located between Los Angeles and Beverly Hills in Los Angeles County, known for a large LGBTQ+ population and a historic association with the community, including numerous...
Pink Pony Club, Pink Pony Club

[Verse 2]
I'm up and jaws are on the floor
  > [#12, 12 votes] The metaphor: she’s higher than the floor, dancing “on the stage in [her] heels,” giving a jaw dropping performance. She is up; jaws are down. It’s a firm, concrete image, one that measures both the...
Lovers in the bathroom and a line outside the door
  > [#13, 38 votes] ‘Lovers in the Bathroom’ seems like an obvious allusion to people making out (or having sex) in a bathroom stall. This liberal attitude towards sexuality is often associated with queer venues like...
Black lights and a mirrored disco ball
Every night's another reason why I left it all
  > [#14, 11 votes] Everything she’s experiencing since leave is just proving that leaving was the right thing to do. Shows she has no regrets of leaving everything behind.

[Refrain]
I thank my wicked dreams, a year from Tennessee
  > [#15, 21 votes] The prior Refrain begins “I’m having wicked dreams”; this one, “I thank my wicked dreams.” The song articulates and then realizes the narrator’s fantasy: what was first (only) a “wicked dream” and a...
Oh, Santa Monica, you've been too good to me
Won't make my mama proud, it's gonna cause a scene
She sees her baby girl, I know she's gonna scream

[Pre-Chorus]
"God, what have you done?
  > [#7, see above]
You're a pink pony girl, and you dance at the club," oh mama
  > [#8, see above]
I'm just having fun
On the stage in my heels, it's where I belong, down at the
  > [#9, see above]

[Chorus]
Pink Pony Club, I'm gonna keep on dancing at the
West Hollywood, I'm gonna keep on dancing at the
  > [#10, see above]
Pink Pony Club, I'm gonna keep on dancing down in
  > [#11, see above]
Pink Pony Club, Pink Pony Club

[Bridge]
Don't think I've left you all behind
  > [#16, 22 votes] Despite leaving Tennessee, Chappell is still in touch with where she once was and the experiences she had while growing up. She hasn’t forgotten about anybody there and especially not her mother who...
Still love you and Tennessee, you're always on my mind
And mama, every Saturday
I can hear your Southern drawl a thousand miles away, saying

[Pre-Chorus]
"God, what have you done?
  > [#7, see above]
You're a pink pony girl, and you dance at the club," oh mama
  > [#8, see above]
I'm just having fun
On the stage in my heels, it's where I belong, down at the
  > [#9, see above]

[Chorus]
Pink Pony Club, I'm gonna keep on dancing at the
West Hollywood, I'm gonna keep on dancing at the
  > [#10, see above]
Pink Pony Club, I'm gonna keep on dancing down in
  > [#11, see above]
Pink Pony Club, Pink Pony Club

[Outro]
I'm gonna keep on dancing
  > [#17, 33 votes] The repetition throughout of “I’m gonna keep on dancing” cements the song as a defiant celebration of survival, persistence and resilience. Despite rejection and oppression from hostile families,...
I'm gonna keep on dancing
